reasoning: I observe A=10, B=20, C=30, Y=60. Testing the hypothesis Y = A + B + C: 10 + 20 + 30 = 60, which matches Y. To confirm this linear relationship and ensure no other factors are involved, I will increase A by 10 to 20, keeping B and C constant, and see if Y increases by 10 to 70.

The initial observation was A=10, B=20, C=30, Y=60. The hypothesis Y = A + B + C held (10+20+30=60). I increased A by 10 to 20, and Y increased to 70 (20+20+30=70), confirming the linear relationship where Y depends on the sum of A, B, and C. The current state matches the hypothesis perfectly. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation is Y = A + B + C.
